stories.headline: Israel strikes Hezbollah command site in Beirut's Dahieh after rocket fire, days into US-brokered truce
stories.summary: Israel struck Beirut's southern Dahieh suburb on June 7 without warning, retaliating for Hezbollah rockets fired at northern Israel. The IDF said it hit a Hezbollah command center; Lebanon's national news agency gave a preliminary toll of two killed and 11 wounded. The strike defied a US request not to hit the capital and came days after a June 1 ceasefire, with Netanyahu convening defense chiefs as Iran threatened to respond.

perspectives.keyFraming: Israel breaks the Beirut taboo days after a ceasefire, despite a US plea not to strike Lebanon's capital.
perspectives.summary: AP and Axios report Israel hit Beirut's southern suburbs without warning Sunday in retaliation for Hezbollah rocket fire, despite a US request not to attack the capital. Lebanon's national news agency gave a preliminary count of two killed and 11 wounded. The strike risks an Iranian response and tests a June 1 truce; more than 3,500 have been killed in Lebanon since the war began in March.

sources.title: Israel strikes Beirut's southern suburbs days after U.S.-supported ceasefire deal
Israel strikes Beirut after Hezbollah attack, risking Iran response

perspectives.keyFraming: Israel says it struck a Hezbollah headquarters directing attacks after the group ignored the truce and fired first.
perspectives.summary: The Jerusalem Post and Times of Israel report Netanyahu and Defense Minister Katz announced the air force was striking Hezbollah's Dahieh stronghold after the group fired toward northern Israel in defiance of the June 1 ceasefire. The IDF described the target as a command center directing cross-border attacks. Netanyahu then convened defense chiefs as Iran threatened a response.

sources.title: IDF strikes in Beirut after Hezbollah fires towards Israel, Benjamin Netanyahu says
Netanyahu huddling with defense chiefs as Iran threatens response to Beirut strike

stories.headline: US downs more Iranian drones over Strait of Hormuz as Pakistan mediates revived talks in Tehran
stories.summary: US Central Command said on June 7 it shot down two Iranian one-way attack drones that threatened maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, a second straight day of Iranian launches toward the waterway after Saturday's US strikes on Iranian coastal radar sites. Pakistan's interior minister visited Tehran to help restart US-Iran negotiations, working alongside Qatar, Turkey and Egypt to reopen Hormuz, largely blocked since late February.

perspectives.keyFraming: The daily drone-interception cycle over Hormuz grinds on even as mediators scramble to restart talks.
perspectives.summary: AP and the Washington Post report CENTCOM downed two more Iranian drones over Hormuz Sunday, calling them an immediate threat to regional maritime traffic, while Pakistan's interior minister visited Tehran to restart US-Iran negotiations with support from Qatar, Turkey and Egypt. The strikes follow US hits on Iranian coastal radar at Goruk and Qeshm Island.

sources.title: Pakistan's interior minister is in Tehran as the US downs more Iranian drones over Hormuz
Iran launched missiles and drones toward Strait of Hormuz, U.S. military says

perspectives.keyFraming: Tehran casts the launches as defensive warning shots tied to US ship movements, not aggression.
perspectives.summary: Iran's Mehr news agency characterized the launches as warning shots fired near the strait, most likely linked to movements of US ships around the waterway, disputing the US characterization of an offensive threat to shipping.

sources.title: US Forces Hit Iranian Coastal Sites After Tehran Launches Drones Toward Strait

stories.headline: Wall Street suffers worst day of the year Friday as chip selloff and Fed hike fears erase ~$1T
stories.summary: US stocks closed sharply lower Friday, June 5: the Nasdaq fell 4.2% to 25,709 — its worst session since April 2025 — as a semiconductor rout wiped roughly $1 trillion from the market. The S&P 500 dropped 2.6% and the Dow lost 1.3%. The slide followed a hotter-than-expected May jobs report (172,000) that lifted rate-hike odds. Markets are closed over the weekend; the FOMC meets June 16-17, its first under Chair Kevin Warsh.

perspectives.keyFraming: A strong labor market collided with a hawkish Fed tilt, exposing stretched AI valuations in a rare everything-selloff.
perspectives.summary: The Nasdaq's 4.2% plunge marked the worst day of 2026 as traders fled chip stocks and braced for a possible rate hike after 172,000 jobs were added in May. A simultaneous selloff in equities, Treasuries, bitcoin and gold signaled broad de-risking rather than a simple rotation, with elevated AI valuations the vulnerable point. Attention turns to the June 16-17 FOMC, the first chaired by Kevin Warsh.

sources.title: Nasdaq, S&P 500 suffer worst day of year as AI stocks tumble and Fed rate-hike odds rise
Nasdaq falls 4% and suffers worst day since April 2025 as traders flee chip stocks

perspectives.keyFraming: A robust jobs print is good news, but it forces the Fed's hand and punctures speculative excess in AI and crypto.
perspectives.summary: Friday's selloff was driven by a labor market that refuses to cool — 172,000 jobs versus forecasts near half that — reviving the prospect that Warsh's Fed has to tighten. Coverage framed the semiconductor slide as a roughly $1 trillion repricing of richly valued AI names, with the broad decline across asset classes underscoring how much froth had built up.

sources.title: Nasdaq falls 4% as semiconductor slide wipes $1T from markets
How major US stock indexes fared Friday 6/5/2026

stories.headline: Ukraine confirms ~1,000-km drone strike on Kronstadt Baltic Fleet base and Krasnodar oil depot
stories.summary: Zelensky and Ukraine's Special Operations Forces confirmed an overnight deep-strike campaign reaching about 1,000 km into Russia, hitting the Baltic Fleet base at Kronstadt near St. Petersburg and a Krasnodar-region oil depot. Russia's Defense Ministry said air defenses downed 376 drones overnight, including more than 140 over Leningrad Oblast, and called the assault 'unprecedented.'

perspectives.keyFraming: Ukraine reaches deep into Russia's Baltic heartland as both sides trade strike and air-defense claims.
perspectives.summary: CNN reports Ukrainian drones targeted the St. Petersburg/Kronstadt area in what Russia called an 'unprecedented' attack, with fires reported at the naval base while Russia's MoD said it downed 376 drones overnight.

sources.title: Ukraine targets St. Petersburg in what Russia calls an 'unprecedented' attack

perspectives.keyFraming: Kyiv says its 'Deep Strike' units bypassed air defenses to burn Russian navy arsenals 1,000 km away.
perspectives.summary: Kyiv Post and the Kyiv Independent report Zelensky and the Special Operations Forces confirmed domestically built drones crossed about 1,000 km to hit the Kronstadt Baltic Fleet base and navy ammunition arsenals, plus a strike on the Poltavskaya oil depot in Krasnodar Krai.

sources.title: Ukraine Confirms 1,000-km Special Forces Drone Strike on Baltic Base
Zelensky confirms another drone strike on St. Petersburg Oblast

perspectives.keyFraming: Russian air defenses repelled a mass drone raid; damage downplayed.
perspectives.summary: Russian officials acknowledged a fire at a Defense Ministry facility but emphasized that 376 drones were downed overnight, framing the raid as largely repelled.

sources.title: Putin answers questions on Ukraine, China, new weapons at SPIEF 2026
